title: Macros
intro: Macros record keystrokes and replay them. Press `q` + a letter to start recording (e.g. `qa`), do your edits, then press `q` again to stop. Replay with `@a`, and prefix a count to repeat: `2@a` runs it twice. Record once, apply everywhere.

exercises.instruction: Record a fix for one line, then apply it to the rest. Press `qa`, then `A;` `Esc` `j` (append a semicolon, leave insert, go down), then `q` to stop — that fixes line 1. Now replay it on the other two lines with `2@a`.
exercises.initialCode:
let a = 1
let b = 2
let c = 3
hints: `qa` ... `q` records into register a; `2@a` replays it twice for the remaining lines.

exercises.instruction: Comment out every line. Record `qa` `I// ` `Esc` `j` `q` (insert `// ` at the line start, then go down), then apply it to the last two lines with `2@a`.
hints: `I` inserts at the first non-blank; type `// ` (with a trailing space) before `Esc`.

exercises.instruction: Record `qa` `A;` `Esc` `j` `q` to fix the first line, then apply it to the remaining three at once with `3@a`.
hints: A count before `@a` replays the macro that many times — `3@a` handles all three remaining lines.

exercises.instruction: Turn every line into a list item: record `qa` `I- ` `Esc` `j` `q` on the first line, then replay with `2@a`.
hints: Type `- ` (dash + space) after `I`, and end the recording with `q` before replaying.

exercises.instruction: Quote every line: record `qa` `I"` `Esc` `A"` `Esc` `j` `q`, then replay with `2@a`.
hints: A macro can enter and leave Insert mode as often as it likes — `I"` for the front, `A"` for the back.
